Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements

Class Clause

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Clause
Direct Known Subclasses:
DisjunctiveClause, EquivalenceClause, FalseClause, TrueClause
public abstract class Clause extends Object
Abstract base class for clauses.

There are four types of clause, the two non-empty clause types EquivalenceClause and DisjunctiveClause and the two empty clause types TrueClause and FalseClause.

Instances of this class are immutable and all accessor methods return an immutable object or a shallow copy of a list of immutable objects.

Equality check and hashcode between clauses using the equals(Object) and hashCode() method does not take into account the origin of a clause for equivalence and disjunctive clause but does for true and false clauses. This is because disjunctive and equivalence clauses are optimised to be used in hashtables. Hashtable.contains(Object) returns true if the hashtable already contains the clause regardless of the origin of the clause.

    • getLevel

      public Level getLevel()

      Returns:
      the level of this clause

    • computeBitSets

      protected abstract void computeBitSets()
      Computes the bit set for predicate literals.

      For each predicate literal, we associate a bit in either the positive literal bit set or the negative literal bit set, corresponding to the index of the predicate literal. Those bit sets are used for checking efficiently whether a predicate literal appears in this clause or not (in the matches(PredicateLiteralDescriptor, boolean) method).

    • matches

      public abstract boolean matches(PredicateLiteralDescriptor predicate, boolean isPositive)
      Returns true if this clause contains a predicate literal that matches the given descriptor with the given sign.

      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate to be matched
      isPositive - the sign of the predicate
      Returns:
      true if this clause contains a predicate literal that matches the given descriptor, false otherwise

    • matchesAtPosition

      public abstract boolean matchesAtPosition(PredicateLiteralDescriptor predicate, boolean isPositive, int position)
      Returns true if the specifed descriptor with the specified sign matches the predicate literal at the specified position in this clause.
      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate to be matched
      isPositive - the sign of that predicate
      position - the position in this clause where the predicate has to match
      Returns:
      true the specifed descriptor with the specified sign matches the predicate literal at the specified position in this clause, false otherwise

    • hasPredicateOfSign

      protected boolean hasPredicateOfSign(PredicateLiteralDescriptor predicate, boolean isPositive)

    • isUnit

      public boolean isUnit()
      Returns true if this clause is a unit clause, i.e. if it contains only one literal, taking conditions into account.
      Returns:
      true if this clause is a unit clause, false otherwise

    • hasQuantifiedLiteral

      public boolean hasQuantifiedLiteral()
      Returns true if this clause has quantified literals (i.e. literals that contain terms that are instances of LocalVariable).
      Returns:
      true if this clause has quantified literals, false otherwise

      public abstract Clause simplify(ISimplifier simplifier)
